meta.title: EU Pay Transparency Directive — Member State Tracker
meta.subtitle: Where the 27 Member States Stand
meta.disclaimer: Post-deadline snapshot for ISO week 2026-W26 (22 June 2026). The 7 June 2026 transposition deadline has passed: only 4 of 27 Member States have a full implementing law in force (Slovakia, Italy, Lithuania, Malta) — some trackers count 3, treating Lithuania's phased law as partial. The European Commission held the deadline firm and signalled no general postponement; no Directive-specific infringement letters had been issued as of late June 2026. This page does not constitute legal advice.

green.label: Transposed or on-track
green.definition: National law adopted, or final bill confirmed to enter into force on or before 7 June 2026.

amber.label: Draft published
amber.definition: Bill or decree in formal consultation or before parliament; intent clear, deadline at risk.

red.label: No public draft / resisting
red.definition: No formal text published, only initial steps announced — or government has signalled non-compliance.

directivePillars: Pay range disclosed in job ads / before interview
Right of employees to information on pay & criteria
Ban on salary-history questions of candidates
Joint pay assessment if unjustified gap > 5%
Reversed burden of proof in equal-pay litigation

directiveSources.title: European Commission confirms 7 June 2026 deadline holds

SK.name: Slovakia
SK.headline: Act No. 76/2026 Coll. in force since 7 Jun 2026 — first EU state to transpose on time
SK.summary: Slovakia's Equal Pay Act (No. 76/2026 Coll.) — adopted by the National Council on 15 Apr 2026, signed by President Pellegrini 23 Apr and published in the Collection of Laws 8 May — entered into force on 7 June 2026 as scheduled. Slovakia is one of only four Member States with a full implementing law in force. First milestones: pay structures by 31 Jul 2026; first gender pay-gap reports (150+ employees) by 7 Jun 2027.

sources.title: Equal Pay Act passed in Slovakia to implement the Gender Pay Transparency Directive
Equal Pay for Men and Women: New Employer Obligations Effective from 7 June 2026

SK.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: confirmed Act No. 76/2026 Coll. in force; no change vs W25.

IT.name: Italy
IT.headline: D.Lgs. 96/2026 published in Gazzetta Ufficiale 1 Jun; in force since 7 Jun 2026
IT.summary: The W21/W22 watch item is resolved: Legislative Decree No. 96 of 7 May 2026 was published in Gazzetta Ufficiale No. 125 on 1 June 2026 and entered into force on 7 June 2026. Italy is one of only four Member States to meet the deadline. Pre-hire pay-disclosure duties and the ban on salary-history questions apply immediately; pay-secrecy clauses are void; gender pay-gap reporting is staggered (≥150 employees by 7 Jun 2027, 100–149 by 7 Jun 2031).

sources.title: Italy Implements the EU Pay Transparency Directive: A Guide to the Final Decree
Pubblicazione in Gazzetta Ufficiale del D.Lgs. n. 96/2026

IT.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: D.Lgs. 96/2026 confirmed in force since 7 Jun; no change vs W25.

MT.name: Malta
MT.headline: Full transposition via Legal Notice 173/2026 on 5 Jun 2026 — met the deadline
MT.summary: Malta completed full transposition on 5 June 2026 with Legal Notice 173 of 2026 (Equal Pay (Transparency and Reporting) Regulations), building on the partial LN 112/2025 in force since 27 Aug 2025. The earlier employer-association push for postponement did not materialise. Workers must receive pay information within 8 days; employers with ≥25 staff must document pay-setting criteria; gender pay-gap reporting applies from 100+ employees. Malta is one of only four Member States to meet the deadline.

sources.title: Malta implements the Pay Transparency Directive
The New Equal Pay Regulations 2026 Published

MT.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: Legal Notice 173/2026 confirmed in force; no change vs W25.

FI.name: Finland
FI.headline: Govt bill (HE) reaching Eduskunta in W25; entry into force now late 2026/early 2027
FI.summary: Finland missed the 7 June deadline. The government proposal amending the Equality Act is reaching the Eduskunta in mid–late June 2026 (Week 25, as flagged in W21), but trackers stop short of confirming a registered HE number. Autumn parliamentary scrutiny is expected, with national entry into force likely late 2026/early 2027. The bill keeps a 100+ threshold for EU public reporting and 30+ for internal pay surveys, with negligence fees of €5,000–€80,000.

sources.title: The Transposition Deadline Has Passed And Most Member States Missed It
Työryhmän esitys palkka-avoimuusdirektiivin täytäntöönpanoksi etenee

FI.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: govt proposal finalised but not yet confirmed tabled in Eduskunta; no status change.

NL.name: Netherlands
NL.headline: Revised bill formally submitted to Tweede Kamer 21 May; targeted entry into force 1 Jan 2027
NL.summary: The Netherlands missed the deadline but advanced procedurally: on 21 May 2026 the minister formally submitted the revised implementation bill (dossier 36949) to the Tweede Kamer, following the Raad van State advice of 7 April. The bill is now before parliament awaiting substantive debate; targeted entry into force remains 1 Jan 2027. The Commission rejected the earlier Dutch postponement request in December 2025.

sources.title: Meer openheid over loonkloof tussen mannen en vrouwen
Wetsvoorstel 36949 – Wet implementatie Richtlijn loontransparantie

NL.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: bill still before Tweede Kamer (submitted 21 May); target 1 Jan 2027. No change.

DK.name: Denmark
DK.headline: Draft targets 1 Jan 2027, but post-election coalition talks add uncertainty
DK.summary: Denmark missed the deadline as planned. The Ministry of Employment's draft (published 26 Feb 2026, consultation closed 27 Mar) still targets entry into force 1 Jan 2027, with first reporting 1 Sep 2028 on 2027 data. The 24 March snap election and subsequent coalition negotiations create explicit uncertainty around that timeline.

sources.title: The Transposition Deadline Has Passed And Most Member States Missed It
Denmark: Transposing the EU Pay Transparency Directive

DK.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: draft targets 1 Jan 2027; no enacted text; no change vs W25.

CZ.name: Czech Republic
CZ.headline: Draft in consultation; deliberate late transposition, act targeted for 1 Jan 2027
CZ.summary: Czechia deliberately chose not to meet the 7 June deadline. The draft Labour Code amendment (published 26 Mar 2026) remains in the legislative process, not yet in the Sbírka zákonů, with a minimalist transposition effective 1 Jan 2027 and reporting/joint-assessment obligations phased to 2028 (150+) and 2031 (100–149).

sources.title: Czechia charts its own course on Pay Transparency Directive transposition
EU Pay Transparency Directive Implementation in the Czech Republic and Slovakia

CZ.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: draft in consultation, targeted 1 Jan 2027; no parliamentary tabling. No change.

PL.name: Poland
PL.headline: Phase-1 binding since Dec 2025; full UC127 act delayed toward early 2027
PL.summary: No new statute by the deadline. Recruitment-stage transparency rules have been binding since 24 Dec 2025, but the full implementing act (UC127) remains in consultation; the Ministry of Family, Labour and Social Policy signals a delayed entry into force with an extended vacatio legis, possibly into early 2027, citing unresolved equality-body provisions.

sources.title: HR in the Know 6/2026: Pay Transparency
7 June 2026 Has Passed – When Will Employees Be Able to Ask About Pay?

PL.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: Phase-1 recruitment rules binding; full UC127 act unenacted, risk of slip to 2027. No change.

RO.name: Romania
RO.headline: Consulted draft predates the 5 May government collapse; path to enactment now uncertain
RO.summary: Romania has a fully consulted draft (published 30 Mar 2026, consultation closed 8 Apr) adding Romania-specific features. The draft predates the collapse of the Bolojan government, removed by a 281-4 no-confidence vote on 5 May 2026; with a limited-powers caretaker in place, the path to enactment is now uncertain. Classified amber on the existence of a complete draft, though progress is politically fragile.

sources.title: Romania revises transposing legislation for EU Pay Transparency Directive
Romania dispatch: Bolojan government removed by successful vote of no confidence

RO.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: revised draft awaiting parliamentary submission; path uncertain. No change.

LT.name: Lithuania
LT.headline: Seimas enacted Law XV-969 on 21 May; Phase 1 in force since 7 Jun 2026
LT.summary: Lithuania moved from committee-endorsed draft to enacted law: the Seimas passed Labour Code amendments (Law No. XV-969) on 21 May 2026, making it one of only four Member States to meet the deadline. Phase 1 provisions — the salary-history ban and rights to pay information — took effect 7 June 2026. Employers must align pay systems by 31 Dec 2026, and monthly data submission to SoDRA (which will calculate the pay-gap metrics) begins 1 Jan 2027. The law exceeds the minimum, e.g. remuneration-policy duties regardless of employer size. Note: most trackers count Lithuania among the four on-time transposers, though some (e.g. DCI Consulting) classify its phased law as partial.

sources.title: Implementation of the EU Pay Transparency Directive in Lithuania
EU Pay Transparency Directive: who actually transposed by 7 June

LT.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: Law XV-969 Phase 1 in force; reporting from 1 Jan 2027. No change vs W25.

LV.name: Latvia
LV.headline: Stand-alone draft cleared consultation 9 Apr; not yet enacted by Saeima — deadline missed
LV.summary: Latvia missed the deadline. The Ministry of Welfare's stand-alone draft cleared consultation on 9 April 2026, but no Cabinet approval or Saeima first reading is confirmed as of mid-June. The draft deviates from the Directive — pay disclosure in job adverts, a narrower 'pay' definition, fewer evaluation criteria, and a 1 June annual reporting deadline.

sources.title: Latvia on track for timely Pay Transparency Directive transposition – with notable deviations
The Transposition Deadline Has Passed And Most Member States Missed It

LV.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: stand-alone draft cleared consultation; not yet enacted by Saeima. No change.

CY.name: Cyprus
CY.headline: Comprehensive draft unadopted; missed deadline, target adoption by Sept 2026
CY.summary: Despite a strong, beyond-minimum draft (criminal sanctions up to 6 months / €10,000, officer liability), Cyprus failed to adopt in time — no enacted law as of mid-June. The bill passed legal-technical review and committees but was not put to the plenary before the deadline, with the 24 May election context contributing to slippage. Target adoption remains 2026.

sources.title: Cyprus sets its course on pay transparency in new draft bill
Did Member States Meet the Deadline? Status of Implementation

CY.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: revised draft in Attorney-General review; not yet at Council of Ministers. No change.

BE.name: Belgium
BE.headline: Missed deadline; federal govt formally requested a 6-month EU extension (~1 Jun)
BE.summary: Belgium missed the 7 June deadline for federal private-sector transposition and, around 1 June, formally wrote to the Commission requesting a six-month postponement and asking it to refrain from infringement action, citing roughly 30 unanswered questions. The Commission has signalled it will not grant a general delay. Only regional public-sector measures (Wallonia–Brussels Federation; Flemish public sector) are adopted.

sources.title: Belgium Seeks Extension for EU Pay Transparency Directive
Belgium requests an additional six months to transpose the Directive

BE.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: 6-month extension request pending; Commission signals no general postponement. No change.

IE.name: Ireland
IE.headline: Formally confirmed (26 May) it will not meet the deadline; phased, no penalties, no Bill
IE.summary: Ireland hardened its non-compliance: in a written answer to a Parliamentary Question on 26 May 2026, the Minister formally confirmed it will not transpose by 7 June — among the first to publicly acknowledge a missed deadline. Implementation will be phased, employers will not be penalised near-term, and the Gender Pay Gap Portal will run for the November 2026 reporting cycle. No transposing Bill is enacted.

keyDates.label: Gender Pay Gap Portal reporting cycle

sources.title: Ireland confirms it will not meet 7 June 2026 transposition deadline
Employers won't be 'penalised' as Ireland to miss deadline

IE.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: confirmed will not meet deadline; no Bill published, off summer priority list. No change.

ES.name: Spain
ES.headline: Prior public consultation closed 8 May; no draft text yet, BOE not before Q3/Q4 2026
ES.summary: Little forward motion. The prior public consultation (consulta pública previa) on a Royal Decree closed 8 May 2026; the draft text is still in drafting, with public-hearing and Council of Ministers phases to come. Spain missed the deadline; BOE publication is expected no earlier than Q3/Q4 2026, with most obligations enforceable in 2027.

sources.title: Spain moves to strengthen pay transparency and implement the EU Directive
Cuenta atrás para la trasposición de la Directiva de Transparencia Retributiva

ES.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: prior consultation closed 8 May; no draft text published; BOE not before Q3/Q4 2026. No change.

SI.name: Slovenia
SI.headline: Inter-ministerial drafting ongoing; no bill registered with Državni zbor — deadline missed
SI.summary: Little movement. No official draft has been published and no bill is registered with the National Assembly; the inter-ministerial working group is still preparing. A partial measure obliging applicant pay information has been in force since 27 Aug 2025, but the bulk of the Directive remains untransposed — placing Slovenia at the amber/red boundary.

sources.title: Pay transparency in Slovenia: upcoming obligations and impact on employers
EU Pay Transparency Directive: who actually transposed by 7 June

SI.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: pre-draft; no bill registered with Državni zbor. No change vs W25.

BG.name: Bulgaria
BG.headline: Draft consultation closed 18 Jun; no bill yet tabled in the National Assembly — deadline missed
BG.summary: Upgrade from red. On 19 May 2026 Bulgaria published a draft transposition amending the Protection Against Discrimination Act and the Labour Code, with public consultation to 18 June. As of 21 June it had not yet had a first reading in the National Assembly; the deadline was missed. CPAD is the monitoring body; reporting phases from 7 Jun 2027 (150+) and 7 Jun 2031 (100–149).

sources.title: Bulgaria's Draft Implementation of the EU Pay Transparency Directive
Bulgaria Publishes Draft Transposition of EU Pay Transparency Directive

BG.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: consultation closed 18 Jun; no bill tabled in the National Assembly yet. Status holds amber.

DE.name: Germany
DE.headline: Missed deadline; still no Referentenentwurf, BMBFSFJ in internal coordination
DE.summary: Germany missed the deadline with no published draft (Referentenentwurf). The deadline lapsed with the existing Entgelttransparenzgesetz unchanged; the BMBFSFJ continues internal coordination, working from the expert commission's final report of 7 Nov 2025 before opening the legislative process. Borderline amber/red — kept red as no bill text exists.

sources.title: Abschlussbericht der Kommission 'Bürokratiearme Umsetzung der Entgelttransparenzrichtlinie'
Germany: The EU Pay Transparency Directive

DE.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: no Referentenentwurf; BMBFSFJ still in internal coordination. No change.

FR.name: France
FR.headline: Missed deadline; draft cleared Conseil d'État, public-sector articles to CCFP 18 Jun; not yet adopted
FR.summary: France missed the deadline but is moving: on 4 June 2026 the Labour Minister sent social partners an expanded second draft (22 articles), with the text going to the Conseil d'État and expected at the Council of Ministers around 25 June. Entry into force would be progressive between late 2026 and 1 January 2028, contingent on parliamentary adoption. No formal bill is yet before parliament — kept red.

sources.title: France Releases an Amended Draft Law to Implement the Pay Transparency Directive
Transparence salariale : le gouvernement dévoile une nouvelle version du projet de loi

FR.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: draft cleared Conseil d'État, public-sector articles to CCFP 18 Jun; Council of Ministers passage not yet occurred. Holds red.

AT.name: Austria
AT.headline: Missed deadline; no Begutachtungsentwurf, transposition via GlBG amendment still pre-draft
AT.summary: Austria missed the deadline with no consultation draft. Transposition is expected via an omnibus amendment to the Gleichbehandlungsgesetz (job postings, pay transparency, income report), but this remains pre-draft. Commentators note many Directive provisions take practical effect from 7 June anyway given the missed deadline.

sources.title: EU-Entgelttransparenz-Richtlinie: Ist sie ab 7. Juni 2026 direkt anwendbar?
EU Pay Transparency Directive: implementation across EU member states

AT.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: no Begutachtungsentwurf; transposition via GlBG still pre-draft. No change.

PT.name: Portugal
PT.headline: No specific transposition law; Govt says text 'being finalised,' broad labour reform in Assembleia
PT.summary: Portugal missed the deadline with no specific transposition law. Around 1 June the Ministry of Labour said transposition was 'being finalised' and would soon enter the legislative circuit. The only vehicle in motion is a broader labour-law reform submitted to the Assembleia, whose EUPTD scope is unclear; the labour inspectorate is reportedly acting on the directive's 'spirit'.

sources.title: Salários mais transparentes? Governo ainda a 'ultimar' transposição da diretiva
The Transposition Deadline Has Passed And Most Member States Missed It

PT.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: no adopted transposition law; party proposals in Labour Committee. No change.

LU.name: Luxembourg
LU.headline: Missed deadline; no bill tabled; parliamentary exchange of views on directive set for 24 Jun
LU.summary: Luxembourg missed the deadline with no projet de loi published. Per the Ministry of Labour (MTEESS), draft legislation is not expected before the second half of 2026, consistent with the assessment that transposition before 2027 is unrealistic.

keyDates.label: Deadline missed, no projet de loi

sources.title: EU Pay Transparency Directive: Key implications and next steps for employers
EU Pay Transparency Directive: What Luxembourg employers must do

LU.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: no projet de loi tabled; Commission du Travail to hold an exchange of views on the directive 24 Jun. Holds red.

HR.name: Croatia
HR.headline: Labour Act PTD amendments still in final prep before the Sabor; no vote yet — deadline missed
HR.summary: Upgrade from red. Labour Minister Alen Ružić said at end-May 2026 that Labour Act (ZOR) amendments transposing the Directive were in final preparation before submission to the Sabor; the March publication target was missed and the legislative process was not complete by 7 June. The text will cover salary ranges in job ads, information rights, and gender pay-gap reporting for larger employers.

sources.title: New EU salary transparency rules begin, Croatia still finalising implementation
EU Pay Transparency Directive: implementation across EU member states

HR.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: correction — no Sabor vote occurred; amendments still in final prep at end-May. Holds amber.

GR.name: Greece
GR.headline: Draft consultation closed 17 Jun; not yet submitted to Parliament for a vote — deadline missed
GR.summary: Significant change from W21 (was red, no text). On 3 June 2026 the Ministry of Labour published a draft transposition law for public consultation, running through 17 June. It incorporates 65 social-partner proposals, gives the Greek Ombudsman a prominent enforcement role, sets a transitional first-reporting period (≥150 employees, to 31 Dec 2026), and triggers joint pay assessment for unjustified gaps ≥5%.

sources.title: Greece unveils draft Pay Transparency law: A new compliance agenda for employers
Gender Pay Equality Bill Opens for Public Consultation

GR.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: consultation closed 17 Jun; not yet submitted to Parliament. Status holds amber.

HU.name: Hungary
HU.headline: No public draft legislation; deadline missed outright
HU.summary: Hungary missed the deadline outright with no publicly available draft. Work is reportedly underway but nothing appears in legal trackers; in the absence of transposing law, existing rules (Labour Code equal-pay provisions, Equal Treatment Act, GDPR) govern in the interim.

sources.title: Hungary may miss deadline for transposing EU's Pay Transparency Directive
Pay Transparency in Hungary: Implementation and HR processes

HU.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: no public draft legislation; deadline missed outright. No change.

SE.name: Sweden
SE.headline: Withdrew its draft (26 Mar) and seeks EU renegotiation; no bill to the Riksdag
SE.summary: Sweden remains the most defiant: after delaying entry into force to 1 Jan 2027, it paused transposition entirely on 26 March 2026, withdrew its proposal, and is pursuing renegotiation/postponement at EU level, citing overlap with the Discrimination Act. No bill has been submitted to the Riksdag; 1 Jan 2027 is only a fallback if renegotiation fails.

keyDates.label: Delayed entry into force to 1 Jan 2027

sources.title: Sweden seeks renegotiation of the Pay Transparency Directive
Sweden will not implement EU Pay Transparency Directive on time

SE.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: no bill to the Riksdag; still seeking EU renegotiation. No change.

EE.name: Estonia
EE.headline: No draft text; minister says Estonia would rather pay a fine than transpose in full
EE.summary: Estonia missed the deadline with no published draft. On 16 April 2026 the Economy Minister (Erkki Keldo) said Estonia would seek postponement and 'would rather pay a fine than meet the deadline,' citing administrative burden, with only limited pre-employment elements signalled. No legislative text has been released.

sources.title: Estonia says no to EU's equal pay directive
The Transposition Deadline Has Passed And Most Member States Missed It

EE.weeklyCheckSummary: W26 check: no draft text; minister prefers a fine over full transposition. No change.
